title: Drivers welcome aid, chafe at rules
desc: Crowds of transport operators packed the registration centre in Bangkok as fuel aid opened, expressing relief but also concern over strict conditions amid surging diesel costs.

title: GH Bank rolls out B4bn mortgage scheme for elderly
desc: Government Housing (GH) Bank is moving forward with its housing loan programme for senior citizens, covering both elderly people who wish to buy a home and those who wish to sell their own homes, with total lending of 4 billion baht.

title: Minister warns of rising price pressure
desc: Commerce Minister Suphajee Suthumpun has acknowledged consumer goods are likely to become more expensive as production costs increase, despite government measures to ease the burden on households.

title: Sultan of Oman keen to visit, boost bilateral ties
desc: The Sultan of Oman has expressed interest in an official visit to Thailand, marking a potential step forward in bilateral relations, says Sihasak Phuangketkeow, the foreign affairs minister.

title: Cancelled flights dent outbound market
desc: Flight cancellations could result in a 15-20% year-on-year decrease in overall outbound trips during the low season, and a 10% decline for the full year.

title: Heatwave boon for beverage makers
desc: Hotter and drier weather than usual, as the global climate shifts towards El Niño, is benefiting Thai beverage makers, even as they face cost pressures from prolonged geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East, analysts say.

title: Thailand banks on EU trade deal
desc: The international trade landscape is changing rapidly, with more barriers and the waning influence of certain established international organisations. In response, countries are reducing their reliance on multilateral systems and strengthening economic stability through bilateral and regional free-trade agreements (FTAs).

title: Thailand driving e-commerce growth
desc: Thailand has emerged as Southeast Asia's e-commerce growth engine amid regional market share consolidation.

title: Arson attacks herald PM visit
desc: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul pledged to accelerate peace efforts in the deep South as suspected insurgents carried out arson attacks believed to be symbolic acts timed with his visit.

title: Enconfund revs up energy projects
desc: The Energy Conservation and Promotion Fund Office (Enconfund) is stepping up efforts to help energy projects move beyond the research stage and reach commercial viability, including an initiative to replace diesel with waste-derived oil.

title: Investors hanging on news of peace talks
desc: Global shares enjoyed a decent run-up while the Thai stock market was closed for the Songkran holidays, with the Nasdaq and S&P500 hitting new highs.

title: SEC sets out key market reforms for 2026
desc: Amid persistent market volatility and mounting external pressures, the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has outlined an ambitious roadmap for the rest of the year, prioritising structural reforms, investor confidence, and proactive risk management.

title: SCB X focuses on tech as conflict drives up costs
desc: SCB X, the financial technology group and holding company of Siam Commercial Bank (SCB), has locked in low-cost technology investments ahead of a broader rise in expenses driven by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

title: Iran declares Hormuz ‘completely open’
desc: Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz “completely open” to commercial shipping on Friday, as a ceasefire came into effect in Lebanon, raising hopes that two of the main obstacles to a US-Iran peace deal may have been cleared.

title: PM and general apologise for latter’s troubling talk
desc: YALA - Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul and the commander of the 4th Army Area have apologised to the public following a controversy over comments by the latter that have triggered calls for his transfer.

title: Pai tourism suffers as haze grips North
desc: Tourist arrivals in Pai have plunged by nearly 90% as hazardous air pollution continues to grip northern Thailand, officials and residents of the town in Mae Hong Son province say.

title: Thais reserve judgement on new cabinet
desc: More than half of Thais are reserving judgement on the new cabinet, with a survey by the King Prajadhipok’s Institute showing 50.8% of respondents do not yet see any group of ministers offering a clear “hope” for the country.

title: Famed Khao Yai tiger dies of natural causes
desc: NAKHON RATCHASIMA - A male Indochinese tiger known as “Sri Kosa”, a key figure in Thailand’s wild tiger population, has died of natural causes in the Dong Phayayen-Khao Yai forest complex, researchers have confirmed.

title: 3,800 litres of untaxed diesel seized off Trat
desc: TRAT - Authorities seized a fishing vessel carrying 3,800 litres of untaxed diesel at a pier in Khlong Yai district of this eastern province early on Friday. The owner admitted he had bought the fuel at sea for 28 to 30 baht per litre for use in the fishing sector.

title: Guru's Weekly Buzz: April 17-23
desc: Guru By Bangkok Post's weekly pick of the most exciting products, activities, food and travel to indulge in.

title: Your horoscope for April 17-23
desc: Your spot-on horoscope for work, money and relationship from Guru by the Bangkok Post's famously accurate fortune teller. Let's see how you will fare this week and beyond.

title: Stella McCartney and H&M releases newest collection with sustainability at its core
desc: Marking a 20-year return since their first collaboration, H&M (Hennes & Mauritz) officially launches a brand-new collection with British fashion label Stella McCartney set to release on May 7.

title: Car trade-in scheme to launch this year with 20,000 quota
desc: The government is set to roll out a car trade-in scheme later this year to encourage motorists to switch to electric and hybrid vehicles, with an initial quota of up to 20,000 units on a first-come, first-served basis, a Finance Ministry source said on Friday.

title: Trump brushes off high fuel costs as ‘fake’ in economic speech
desc: LAS VEGAS — United States President Donald Trump on Thursday dismissed high fuel costs amid his war with Iran, claiming that the economic damage inflicted by the war was much less severe than expected.

title: 10年追逃落幕 中国传销案嫌犯在曼谷被捕
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，2011年10月至2014年6月期间，中国籍男子费某（化名）与他人共同实施诈骗活动，运营名为“1040”的传销项目。该项目以高额回报为诱饵，通过口口相传、封闭式聚会等方式进行宣传，诱骗受害者加入，并向每人收取69800元人民币（约349000泰铢）作为会员费。 随后，组织通过发展下线，构建多层级结构，并以成员数量及等级差异作为返利依据。同时承诺，最高级别成员可获得高达1040万元人民币（约5200万泰铢）的回报。费某作为组织发起人及主要负责人之一，负责成员数量统计及资金管理。案发时，该组织成员已超过300人，涉案金额累计约超过1亿泰铢。 此后，中国公安部对其签发逮捕令，并吊销其护照，同时协调泰国方面协助追捕。在接到通报后，泰国移民局在帕努玛警上校等指挥下，责成移民局第一分局调查组展开追踪行动。 经调查发现，费某已潜逃至泰国，在曼谷居住并经营业务。 4月17日约10时，泰国警方前往现场核查，确认其身份后，依法撤销其在泰国的居留许可。鉴于其涉嫌诈骗罪，警方已向其说明相关情况，并将其移送相关部门依法处理。 （编译：hansa ；审核：wan；） The post 10年追逃落幕 中国传销案嫌犯在曼谷被捕 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: 泰娱最甜CP成真！Nadech与Yaya16年爱情长跑终成眷属
desc: 泰国头条新闻社 据泰媒报道，4月17日，泰国超人气情侣Nadech与Yaya大婚首日成为全民关注焦点。此前两人已抵达Nadech 的家乡孔敬府，准备举行盛大婚礼。这对深受大众喜爱的情侣终于步入婚姻殿堂，全国粉丝都为之欣喜。 与此同时，网络上也掀起对两人爱情历程的关注与讨论。 Nadech与Yaya初次相识于电视剧《心之爱焰》，该剧属于4部系列剧之一，于2010年在泰国3台播出。剧中Nadech饰演Fai，Yaya饰演Jeed，两人强烈的化学反应迅速引发荧幕情侣（CP）热潮。 此后，两人合作不断，包括《爱与罚》、《筑梦庄园之心火》 、《逐爱天涯》、《换身密谋》、《娜迦2》、 《命中注定我爱你》、《金娜丽花纹》等作品。尽管当时对外关系仍被称为“亲密兄妹”、“知己”，但粉丝早已开始期待他们发展为真实情侣。后来Nadech坦言，在《筑梦庄园之心火》时期已开始追求Yaya，但选择低调发展。 虽然尚未正式公开恋情，但两人常一起做功德、互相关照，关系稳定而温和。直到2019年，在Mai 出道30周年演唱会“I AM MAI”上，Nadech作为嘉宾惊喜告白Yaya：“她是我的爱人”，引发全场轰动。 随后，两人的感情逐渐公开。2021年情人节，Nadech在曼谷Central World投放广告表白，引发全城热议。 2023年6月，Nadech在意大利Positano海边浪漫求婚成功，Yaya随即

title: 'AI shamans' tell the fortunes of curious South Koreans
desc: SEOUL — The sound of tinkling bells drifts through an alley in central Seoul, an unmistakable sign that a shaman is near -- although in this case the mystic is a robot powered by artificial intelligence (AI)

title: 3 foreign men assault another foreigner in Pattaya Songkran celebration
desc: 3 foreign men assault another foreigner in Pattaya Songkran celebration Legacy A video recently shared online showed a group of foreign men in a fight during Songkran celebrations on Pattaya Walking Street on April 17. The footage, posted by a Thai woman, showed a group of three men, believed to be from Middle Eastern countries, confronting a foreign man reported to be European, aged between 50 and 60. In the caption, the woman wrote… “Arab tourists caused problems again. Three of them attacked an elderly foreign man in a gang attack. The three men had initially argued with another

title: Why investors are choosing Bangkok property over Singapore and Hong Kong in 2026
desc: Why investors are choosing Bangkok property over Singapore and Hong Kong in 2026 Legacy For a US$500,000 (17.5 million baht) budget, a buyer in Singapore’s Core Central Region walks away with roughly 20 square metres of property. The same budget at the Hong Kong Peak gets around 10. In prime Bangkok property along Sukhumvit, that same amount buys approximately 80 square metres of freehold condo, with change to spare. That contrast is why Chinese, Hong Kong, Singaporean and Middle Eastern buyers keep choosing Bangkok over its regional rivals. The Thai capital is the only major Asian city

title: Trat fisherman fined over 140,000 baht for illegal diesel
desc: Trat fisherman fined over 140,000 baht for illegal diesel Legacy Excise officers in Trat and marine police seized 3,800 litres of illegal diesel from a fishing boat in Khlong Yai in the early hours of today, April 17, after receiving a tip-off about untaxed fuel entering Thai waters. At about 1am, officers searched the vessel at Por Kasemsiri pier in Khlong Yai district, Trat. Surachai, a 62 year old man who identified himself as the boat owner, led officers during the inspection. Inside the boat, officers found 19 drums of untaxed diesel, each with a capacity of 200 litres, for a total of 3,8

title: Bangkok woman claims date rape drug was sprayed into her face at Songkran party
desc: Bangkok woman claims date rape drug was sprayed into her face at Songkran party Legacy A Thai woman issued a public warning after alleging that she was exposed to gamma-hydroxybutyrate (GHB), or date rape drug , through water sprayed from a water gun during a Songkran event at an entertainment venue in Bangkok . The TikTok user, @jeeppji, shared a photo of herself unconscious in the hospital on Monday, April 13. In the caption, she wrote that she was drugged with GHB at a Songkran event at Route 66 and warned others to be careful. GHB inherited the date rape drug title as it is often misused f

title: Stranded in Thailand after Songkran? Here is what to do
desc: Stranded in Thailand after Songkran? Here is what to do Legacy Songkran is one of the biggest travel periods in Thailand, and when it ends, millions of people try to leave at the same time. In 2026, that pressure has combined with a series of disruptions, Middle East airspace closures, water-damaged passports, financial strain, and unexpected legal complications, leaving a significant number of tourists stranded in Thailand after Songkran with no clear exit. If that is you, here is what you are dealing with and what to do about it. Getting stranded in Thailand during Songkran is more common th

title: 油价持续上涨，泰航宣布减少5月航班
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月17日，泰国国际航空公司（简称“泰航”）宣布调整2026年5月的航班安排，减少国内、东盟、北亚、南亚及欧洲的飞行班次。泰航表示，此次调整的主要原因是燃油成本的持续上升，且5月非旅游旺季，旅客需求有所放缓。具体如下： 泰国国内航线： – 曼谷-孔敬：由每天4班减至3班，全月执行 – 曼谷-乌隆：由每天3班减至2班，全月执行 – 曼谷-乌汶：由每周14班减至12班，5月5日-28日期间执行 东盟航线： – 曼谷-柬埔寨金边：由每周14班减至11班，5月2日-30日期间执行 – 曼谷-新加坡：由每天5班减至4班，5月2日-31日期间执行 北亚航线： – 曼谷-日本东京成田：由每天3班减至2班，5月11日-31日期间执行，29日除外 – 曼谷-中国北京：由每天2班减至1班，5月7日-31日期间执行，29日除外 – 曼谷-中国上海：由每天2班减至1班，5月11日-31日期间执行，30日除外 – 曼谷-韩国首尔仁川：由每天3班减至1班，5月8日-31日期间执行 – 曼谷-中国台湾台北：由每天3班减至2班，5月6日-31日期间执行 – 曼谷-中国香港：由每天4班减至3班，5月6日-31日期间执行 – 曼谷-中国台湾高雄：5月8日-3

title: Chinese man killed after falling under truck in Pattaya
desc: Chinese man killed after falling under truck in Pattaya Legacy A 48 year old Chinese man died in a Pattaya motorcycle accident yesterday, April 16, after losing control of his motorbike and being run over by a six-wheel water truck . Police were called at around 12pm to a road near a school in Nong Prue subdistrict, Bang Lamung district, where a truck had collided with a motorcycle . Officers from Nong Prue Police Station and rescue workers arrived to find a damaged white Honda motorbike at the scene. The rider, identified as Yang Wang, was found trapped near the vehicle and pronounced dead at

title: 超30万人参与，泰国“超级宋干节”吸金4.48余亿
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月17日，泰国国家旅游局透露，4月11日至15日在曼谷班嘉琦缇公园举行的2026年“超级宋干节”（Maha Songkran World Water Festival）吸引了全球300,878人参与，包括泰国本地123,664人、外国游客177,214人；创造经济循环超过4.48亿泰铢，其中活动现场消费约8,530万泰铢，周边旅游相关消费约3.628亿泰铢，充分体现出宋干节在刺激经济和多领域收入方面的重要作用，彰显了泰国作为世界级文化遗产与传统节日目的地的地位。 泰国国家旅游局局长塔佩妮表示，感谢所有泰国民众对外国游客的热情接待和细心照顾，在以适当方式传承宋干节传统的同时，为游客带来难忘美好的体验，让他们想再次来到泰国。旅游业者和政府相关部门也全力合作，提供便利的交通服务和安全保障，为泰国旅游形象加分，给世界各地的游客留下深刻且积极的印象。 不少国际媒体实时报道了“超级宋干节”的庆祝活动，且纷纷称赞泰国宋干节是全球最具规模、最有活力的大型活动之一，展示出泰国文化的强大力量。国家旅游局将继续推行旅游刺激措施，维持旅游热潮，并重点关注游客的体验价值，结合“价值重于数量”（Value over Volume）的政策方向和“新泰国”（The New Thailand）的理念，推动泰国成为可持续发展的高质量旅游目的地。 在5天活动期间，1,200多架无人机带来的灯光秀最受

title: Yala boy with special needs found dead in scrapyard car after four-day search
desc: Yala boy with special needs found dead in scrapyard car after four-day search Legacy A 12 year old Thai boy with special needs was found dead in a scrap car about 800 metres away, four days after he went missing from his home in Yala province. The 38 year old mother, Wanida, reported the disappearance of her son, Yuttasak, also known as Best, to Yarom Police Station on April 12 after the boy went missing from their home in Soi Sub Anan in the Betong district in the afternoon on the day. Wanida told police that Best was a child with special needs who often played in the forest near the house or

title: Thailand set for slowest economic growth in ASEAN in 2026, IMF says
desc: Thailand set for slowest economic growth in ASEAN in 2026, IMF says Legacy Thailand is expected to record the slowest economic growth in ASEAN in 2026 after the International Monetary Fund (IMF) cut its forecast to 1.5% , down from 1.6% projected in January. The downgrade, reported today, April 17, places Thailand behind its regional peers , including Cambodia, Laos and Myanmar. The IMF said Vietnam is expected to lead the region with 7.1% growth, followed by Indonesia at 5%, Malaysia at 4.7%, the Philippines at 4.1% and Singapore at 3.5%. Cambodia and Laos are each forecast to grow by around

title: 2 transgender suspects surrender after leaving hickey on victim’s neck
desc: 2 transgender suspects surrender after leaving hickey on victim’s neck Legacy Two from three transgender suspects surrendered to police afte r sexually assaulting a man during Songkran celebrations in Phichit province, leaving a hickey on his neck. The 24 year old victim, Chitsanupong, filed a complaint with Mueang Phichit Police Station after the incident on April 15 at a Songkran event on Khao Tang Road. He had earlier shared details of the Phichit Songkran sexual assault on his Facebook account. Chitsanupong said three to four people approached him during the event, applied powder to

title: British pensioner dies after being swept out to sea in Hua Hin
desc: British pensioner dies after being swept out to sea in Hua Hin Legacy A British pensioner from Liverpool died after being swept out to sea in Hua Hin, Prachuap Khiri Khan, on April 15. Police said he was swimming alone and was unable to return to shore . The 75 year old man had entered the water in the morning as his girlfriend watched from the beach. His body was later found by a fisherman near a pier at about 4.30pm. Police Captain Prasong Jaturat said the man was a widower and that his family lives in England, where he had been living with his children. Photo via The Sun Prasong said the ma

title: 泰国宋干节7天严管期酒驾案件5806起 同比下降4.82%
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 4月17日，泰国缓刑署署长皮亚表示，宋干节期间严管期执法工作持续推进。4月16日为该措施的最后一天，仍有案件持续进入缓刑监管程序，反映出相关部门执法之严格。 仅4月16日当天进入缓刑监管程序案件即948起，其中酒驾案件861起，占90.82%；毒驾案件85起，占8.96%；危险驾驶案件1起，占0.11%；飙车案件1起，占0.11%。整个严管期间进入缓刑监管程序案件总计6180起。其中酒驾案件5806起，占93.95%；毒驾案件366起，占5.92%；危险驾驶案件5起，占0.08%；飙车案件3起，占0.05%。对比2025年宋干节期间酒驾案件6100起，2026年酒驾案件为5806起，同比减少294起，降幅为4.82%。按地区统计，酒驾案件数量最高的3个地区分别为：清迈府757起、北榄府495起、曼谷351起。 皮亚表示，严管期结束后，全国工作人员将加快对违法者背景记录的细致核查，并严格执行法律措施，重点筛查酒精相关高风险行为人员，纳入系统化矫正与复健流程，并按情况转介接受相应治疗。同时，将持续对酒驾违法者开展行为矫正活动，提高其对生命与社会影响的认知，强化道路安全意识。 过去7天，全国缓刑机构与各方合作持续开展社会活动，包括在177个便民服务点组织社区服务活动，参与人数2574人；开展安全用路宣传活动18次，参与人数1643人；在事故高风险区域开展环境整治14次，

title: 素万那普机场航司数量全球登顶 最佳机场排名猛涨
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，4月17日，地图数据平台Brilliant Map发布最新统计称，素万那普机场以113家航空公司执飞的规模，被列为全球航司数量最多的机场第1位。 该机场在相关指标上已超过巴黎、迪拜、伊斯坦布尔、罗马、米兰、上海、东京、新加坡和法兰克福等全球主要航空枢纽。 除上述统计外，素万那普机场在全球最佳机场排名中升至第39位，较此前的第58位有所上升。泰国交通部提出目标，计划在5年内推动素万那普机场进入全球前20，并拟扩建东侧航站楼及南侧区域，以提升容量至年吞吐1.2亿人次，同时引入技术手段缓解拥堵、提升通行效率。 报道还称，泰方将推进对全国主要机场的升级计划，包括廊曼、清迈、普吉、清莱等，以应对游客增长并带动泰国旅游经济发展。 （编译：hansa；审核：wan；来源：thaiger） The post 素万那普机场航司数量全球登顶 最佳机场排名猛涨 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .

title: 泰国登顶亚洲 全球第8！国际榜单点名文化遗产含金量
desc: 泰国头条新闻社讯 据泰媒报道，4月17日，《美国新闻与世界报道》发布2024年文化遗产最丰富国家相关排名。在覆盖全球89个国家和地区的评比中，泰国被列为亚洲地区第1位、全球第8位。 本次评比综合考量多项因素，包括悠久历史、文化类旅游资源、对本地文化的可接近性、地理吸引力，以及泰国饮食在全球范围的知名度与接受度等。 报道指出，泰国在现代化发展与文化根基之间形成较强融合度，海外游客对历史地标、寺庙建筑、海滩景观以及泰式传统按摩等特色体验保持较高关注度。相关要素也被视为推动泰国旅游经济的重要支点。 （编译：hansa；审核：wan；来源：thaiger） The post 泰国登顶亚洲 全球第8！国际榜单点名文化遗产含金量 appeared first on 泰国头条新闻 .
